question: Which countries have the highest rice yield per hectare, and how does their total rice production compare with those having the greatest production volumes?

analysis.design_rationale: A bubble scatter plot allows us to visually compare two important metrics for each country: rice yield per hectare vs. total rice production. By encoding yield on the y-axis, production on the x-axis, and area cultivated (Rice Acreage (Hectare)) as the bubble's size, the plot highlights whether high yield countries are also dominant in overall production and acreage – an insight not visible in rankings alone. Color encodes yield rank for added depth.
analysis.target_insight: This visualization reveals the relationship (or potential disconnect) between rice yield and total production, and shows whether countries with high yield are also those with largest farming area or greatest tonnage produced.

chart_encodings.color: Rank of Rice Yield (Viridis colormap inverted so rank 1 = brightest)
chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: Bubble labels for top 10 in yield; transparency (alpha=0.7) for visual balance

transformations: Convert 'Rice Yield (Kg / Hectare)', 'Rice Production (Tons)', and 'Rice Acreage (Hectare)' from string to numeric (handle 'K' and 'M')
Drop rows with missing/invalid numeric values
Invert 'Rank of Rice Yield' for the colormap (rank 1 = brightest)
Select all countries for complete global comparison
Label bubbles for top 10 yield countries
data_preparation_steps: df = df.dropna(subset=['Rice Yield (Kg / Hectare)','Rice Production (Tons)','Rice Acreage (Hectare)','Rank of Rice Yield'])
rank_inv = df['Rank of Rice Yield'].max() - df['Rank of Rice Yield'] + 1
suggestion.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib as mpl

df = pd.read_csv('csv_c_squared\3.csv')
df['Rice Yield (Kg / Hectare)'] = df['Rice Yield (Kg / Hectare)'].str.replace('K','000').str.replace('M','000000').astype(float)
df['Rice Production (Tons)'] = df['Rice Production (Tons)'].str.replace('K','000').str.replace('M','000000').astype(float)
df['Rice Acreage (Hectare)'] = df['Rice Acreage (Hectare)'].str.replace('K','000').str.replace('M','000000').astype(float)
df = df.dropna(subset=['Rice Yield (Kg / Hectare)','Rice Production (Tons)','Rice Acreage (Hectare)','Rank of Rice Yield'])
rank_inv = df['Rank of Rice Yield'].max() - df['Rank of Rice Yield'] + 1
cmap = mpl.colormaps['viridis']
colors = cmap(rank_inv / rank_inv.max())
sizes = np.sqrt(df['Rice Acreage (Hectare)'])/np.sqrt(df['Rice Acreage (Hectare)'].max())*1200
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10,8))
sc = ax.scatter(df['Rice Production (Tons)'], df['Rice Yield (Kg / Hectare)'], s=sizes, c=colors, alpha=0.7, edgecolors='k')
for idx, row in df.sort_values('Rank of Rice Yield').head(10).iterrows():
    ax.text(row['Rice Production (Tons)'], row['Rice Yield (Kg / Hectare)'], row['Country'], fontsize=9, va='bottom', ha='right', color='darkblue')
sm = mpl.cm.ScalarMappable(cmap=cmap, norm=mpl.colors.Normalize(vmin=1, vmax=df['Rank of Rice Yield'].max()))
sm.set_array([])
cbar = plt.colorbar(sm, ax=ax, orientation='vertical', pad=0.02)
cbar.set_label('Rank of Rice Yield (1 = Highest)', fontsize=12)
ax.set_xlabel('Rice Production (Tons)', fontsize=13)
ax.set_ylabel('Rice Yield (Kg / Hectare)', fontsize=13)
plt.title('Rice Yield vs. Total Production\nBubble size = Acreage, Color = Yield Rank', fontsize=14)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
